title: विन्ध्येश्वरी चालीसा
devanagariText:
॥ श्री विन्ध्येश्वरी चालीसा ॥

॥ दोहा ॥
जय विन्ध्याचल विहारिणी जय माँ जगदम्बा।
सकल सिद्धि दायिनी तुम्हीं शरण हम अम्बा॥

॥ चौपाई ॥
जय जय जय विन्ध्येश्वरी माता।
सकल जगत की तुम विधाता॥
विन्ध्याचल पर्वत पर वासा।
जहँ होत सदा तव जय जयकारा॥
त्रिगुणमयी त्रिभुवनेश्वरी।
महालक्ष्मी महासरस्वती अम्बा महेश्वरी॥
ब्रह्मा विष्णु शंकर जग जाने।
तव महिमा को कोई न जाने॥
शुम्भ निशुम्भ दैत्य तुम मारे।
रक्तबीज को रण संहारे॥
महिषासुर का किया विनाशा।
सुर नर मुनि सबकी पूरी आशा॥
चण्ड मुण्ड संहारन हारी।
जय माँ चामुण्डा भयनिवारी॥
धूम्रलोचन असुर को मारा।
देवन को तुमने तब उबारा॥
ब्रह्माणी रुद्राणी तुम अम्बा।
वैष्णवी शक्ति जगदम्बा॥
नवरात्रि में पूजन तेरा।
दूर हो सब दुख अन्धेरा॥
शैलपुत्री ब्रह्मचारिणी माता।
चन्द्रघण्टा कूष्माण्डा विधाता॥
स्कन्दमाता कात्यायनी कहाई।
कालरात्रि महागौरी सुखदाई॥
सिद्धिदात्री नवदुर्गा नाम।
जपते पूरे हों सब काम॥
सिंहवाहिनी अष्टभुजा धारी।
त्रिशूल खड्ग सबको भयहारी॥
शंख चक्र गदा पद्म सुहावन।
धनुष बाण तुमको है भावन॥
रक्तवर्ण और गौर शरीरा।
जटामुकुट शोभित तन वीरा॥
नरमुण्ड की माला विराजे।
तेज प्रताप सब जग में गाजे॥
विन्ध्य क्षेत्र में महिमा न्यारी।
सब सिद्ध पीठ में तुम अधिकारी॥
भक्तन को वरदान तुम देतीं।
सन्तन पर सदा स्नेह करतीं॥
जो कोई ध्यान धरे तव माता।
दुःख दरिद्र मिटे सब त्राता॥
रोग शोक भय क्लेश निवारो।
संकट से जन को तुम तारो॥
हम सब उतरें भवसागर।
कृपा करो जगदम्बा सुन्दर॥
विन्ध्याचल की शोभा न्यारी।
जहँ माँ तुम विराजो सुखकारी॥
भक्ति भाव से जो तुम्हें ध्यावे।
सो सब सुख सम्पत्ति को पावे॥
पुत्र दायिनी सुख की खानी।
जय जय जय विन्ध्येश्वरी भवानी॥
सदा सुमंगल दायिनी माता।
तुम बिन कोई हमारा न त्राता॥

॥ दोहा ॥
विन्ध्येश्वरी चालीसा पढ़े भक्ति तन लाय।
सकल मनोरथ सिद्ध हो माँ का आशीष पाय॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Vindyeshwari Chalisa ||

|| Doha ||
Jaya Vindhyachala Viharini Jaya Maa Jagadamba.
Sakala Siddhi Dayini Tumhi Sharan Hum Amba.

|| Chaupai ||
Jaya Jaya Jaya Vindyeshwari Mata.
Sakala Jagat Ki Tum Vidhata.
Vindhyachala Parvat Par Vasa.
Jahan Hot Sada Tava Jaya Jayakara.
Trigunamayi Tribhuvaneshwari.
Mahalakshmi Mahasaraswati Amba Maheshwari.
Brahma Vishnu Shankar Jag Jane.
Tava Mahima Ko Koi Na Jane.
Shumbh Nishumbh Daitya Tum Mare.
Raktabij Ko Ran Samhare.
Mahishasur Ka Kiya Vinasha.
Sur Nar Muni Sabki Puri Asha.
Chand Mund Samharan Hari.
Jaya Maa Chamunda Bhayanivari.
Dhumralochana Asur Ko Mara.
Devan Ko Tumne Tab Ubara.
Brahmani Rudrani Tum Amba.
Vaishnavi Shakti Jagadamba.
Navratri Mein Pujan Tera.
Dur Ho Sab Dukh Andhera.
Shailputri Brahmacharini Mata.
Chandraghanta Kushmanda Vidhata.
Skandamata Katyayani Kahai.
Kalaratri Mahagauri Sukhdai.
Siddhidatri Navdurga Naam.
Japte Pure Hon Sab Kaam.
Sinhavahini Ashtabhuja Dhari.
Trishul Khadg Sabko Bhayahari.
Shankh Chakra Gada Padma Suhavan.
Dhanush Baan Tumko Hai Bhavan.
Raktavarna Aur Gaur Sharira.
Jatamukut Shobhit Tan Vira.
Narmund Ki Mala Viraje.
Tej Pratap Sab Jag Mein Gaje.
Vindhya Kshetra Mein Mahima Nyari.
Sab Siddh Pith Mein Tum Adhikari.
Bhaktan Ko Vardan Tum Deti.
Santan Par Sada Sneh Karti.
Jo Koi Dhyan Dhare Tav Mata.
Dukh Daridra Mite Sab Trata.
Rog Shok Bhay Klesh Nivaro.
Sankat Se Jan Ko Tum Taro.
Hum Sab Utren Bhavsagar.
Kripa Karo Jagdamba Sundar.
Vindhyachal Ki Shobha Nyari.
Jahan Maa Tum Virajo Sukhkari.
Bhakti Bhav Se Jo Tumhen Dhyave.
So Sab Sukh Sampatti Ko Pave.
Putra Dayini Sukh Ki Khani.
Jaya Jaya Jaya Vindyeshwari Bhavani.
Sada Sumangal Dayini Mata.
Tum Bin Koi Hamara Na Trata.

|| Doha ||
Vindyeshwari Chalisa Padhe Bhakti Tan Lay.
Sakala Manorath Siddh Ho Maa Ka Ashish Pay.
hindiMeaning:
हे विन्ध्याचल में विहार करने वाली! हे जगदम्बा माँ! आपकी जय हो! आप सकल सिद्धि देने वाली हैं, हम आपकी शरण में हैं।

आप विन्ध्याचल पर्वत पर निवास करती हैं। आप त्रिगुणमयी, त्रिभुवनेश्वरी, महालक्ष्मी, महासरस्वती और महेश्वरी हैं। आपने शुम्भ-निशुम्भ, रक्तबीज, महिषासुर, चण्ड-मुण्ड और धूम्रलोचन का संहार किया।

आप नवदुर्गा रूप में शैलपुत्री से सिद्धिदात्री तक सब रूपों में पूजित हैं। आप सिंहवाहिनी, अष्टभुजा, त्रिशूल-खड्ग धारिणी हैं।

जो भक्तिभाव से आपका ध्यान करे, उसके सब दुःख-दरिद्र मिट जाते हैं और सब सुख-सम्पत्ति प्राप्त होती है।
benefits: Invokes the blessings of Goddess Vindhyavasini, presiding deity of Vindhyachal
Invokes fulfillment of all desires and wishes
Traditionally recited for removal of poverty, diseases, sorrows, and fears
Invokes the blessing of children, prosperity, and auspiciousness
Provides protection from all enemies and evil forces
description: The 40 chaupais give the Chalisa its name (chalis = forty); framing dohas bring the full recitation to 43 verse-units. Vindyeshwari Chalisa (विन्ध्येश्वरी चालीसा) is a sacred 40-verse devotional hymn dedicated to Goddess Durga. Rooted in Traditional, this composition carries deep spiritual significance. Regular recitation is believed to provide protection from all enemies and evil forces, invoke the blessings of Goddess Vindhyavasini, presiding deity of Vindhyachal, and bestow children, prosperity, and auspiciousness. For best results, devotees chant this on Tuesday (Mangalvar) and Friday (Shukravar) and during Navratri. With 40 verses and a reading time of about 12 minutes, this prayer is ideal for both daily worship and special occasions. On this page, you can read the complete Vindyeshwari Chalisa in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Vindyeshwari Chalisa in Hindi with English transliteration and meaning. Prayer to Goddess Vindhyavasini for blessings and protection.

seoFaqs.question: Who is Maa Vindyeshwari and where is her main temple?
seoFaqs.answer: Maa Vindyeshwari, also called Vindhyavasini ('she who dwells in the Vindhyas'), is a fierce form of Adi Shakti enshrined at Vindhyachal in Mirzapur district, Uttar Pradesh, on the banks of the Ganga. She is traditionally identified as the Yogamaya — the goddess who was substituted for the infant Krishna in Kamsa's prison and ascended to the Vindhya mountains, taunting Kamsa as recorded in the Bhagavata and Devi Bhagavata Puranas. Vindhyachal is one of the most important Devi pilgrimage sites in North India and is reckoned among the Shakti Peethas. The pilgrimage circuit (Trikon Parikrama) connects three temples — Vindhyavasini, Ashtabhuja Devi, and Kali Khoh — each representing one of the three goddess forms. (Source: Vindhyachal Mandir; Bhakti Information)

seoFaqs.question: What is the Trikon Parikrama at Vindhyachal?
seoFaqs.answer: The Trikon Parikrama is the unique three-corner pilgrimage circuit at Vindhyachal, in which devotees visit the three principal Devi shrines in sequence to obtain the complete blessing of Adi Shakti. The circuit covers Maa Vindhyavasini (the central shrine, representing Mahalakshmi), Maa Ashtabhuja Devi (representing Mahasaraswati, located on a hill west of the main temple), and Maa Kali Khoh (representing Mahakali, set inside a cave to the east). The complete parikrama spans roughly 5 km and is traditionally performed barefoot, with the Vindyeshwari Chalisa recited at each shrine. The triadic structure mirrors the Mahakali-Mahalakshmi-Mahasaraswati grouping seen at Vaishno Devi, and devotees regard the three shrines as a single integrated Devi yantra. (Source: Vindhyachal Mandir)

seoFaqs.question: What does the opening verse 'Jai Jai Jai Vindhyachal Rani' praise?
seoFaqs.answer: The well-known opening — 'Jai Jai Jai Vindhyachal Rani / Aadi Shakti jag vidit Bhavani / Singhvahini jai jagmata / Jai jai jai tribhuvan sukhdata' — translates as: 'Victory, victory, victory to the Queen of Vindhyachal, the primordial Shakti known throughout the world as Bhavani, the lion-rider, mother of the universe, giver of joy to all three worlds.' These four lines lay out the goddess's four key identities: regional sovereign (Vindhyachal Rani), cosmic principle (Adi Shakti), warrior on a lion (Singhvahini), and universal mother (Jagmata). The verse is sung in Hindi-Brij dialect, making it accessible to the predominantly Hindi-speaking pilgrim base of Mirzapur, Varanasi, Allahabad, and Bihar. (Source: Bhakti Nidhi; Sri Mandir)

seoFaqs.question: Why are Tuesdays, Fridays, and Navratri the best times to recite Vindyeshwari Chalisa?
seoFaqs.answer: As with all major Devi chalisas, the most auspicious period is Navratri — both the Chaitra and Sharad Navratri — when the Vindhyachal temple complex draws lakhs of pilgrims and special abhishek and havans are performed. In the weekly cycle, Tuesday (the day of Mars and warrior-Devi worship) and Friday (the day of Mahalakshmi and Shukra) are considered most efficacious for Vindyeshwari Chalisa recitation. Many devotees recite the chalisa 108 times during Navratri or perform a 9-day anushthan with akhand jyot. Reciting it in the early morning hours (Brahma-muhurta, 4-6 AM) is traditionally recommended for maximum benefit, particularly for relief from enemies, court cases, and persistent obstacles. (Source: Astromantra; Bhakti Information)
